L. 104–193“(2)State and local government electronic benefit transfer programs.—“(A) .—The disclosures, protections, responsibilities, and remedies established under this subchapter, and any regulation prescribed or order issued by the Board in accordance with this subchapter, shall not apply to any electronic benefit transfer program established under State or local law or administered by a State or local government.Exemption generally“(B) .—Subparagraph (A) shall not apply with respect to any electronic funds transfer under an electronic benefit transfer program for deposits directly into a consumer account held by the recipient of the benefit.Exception for direct deposit into recipient’s account“(C) .—No provision of this paragraph may be construed as—Rule of construction“(i) affecting or altering the protections otherwise applicable with respect to benefits established by Federal, State, or local law; or“(ii) otherwise superseding the application of any State or local law.“(D) .—For purposes of this paragraph, the term ‘electronic benefit transfer program’—Electronic benefit transfer program defined“(i) means a program under which a government agency distributes needs-tested benefits by establishing accounts to be accessed by recipients electronically, such as through automated teller machines, or point-of-sale terminals; and“(ii) does not include employment-related payments, including salaries and pension, retirement, or unemployment benefits established by Federal, State, or local governments.”, designated existing provisions as par. (1), inserted subsec. heading and par. (2), and substituted “If” for “In the event that”.Pub. L. 104–193, § 891

children.heading: Prescription by the Bureau and the Board
children.label: a Prescription by the Bureau and the Board

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Except as provided in paragraph (2), the Bureau shall prescribe rules to carry out the purposes of this subchapter.

children.label: A
children.text: to carry out the purposes of this subchapter with respect to a person described in ; andsection 5519(a) of title 12

children.label: B
children.text: to carry out the purposes of section 1693–2 of this title.o

consult with the other agencies referred to in section 1693 of this title and take into account, and allow for, the continuing evolution of electronic banking services and the technology utilized in such services,12So in original. See 2010 Amendment note below.1See References in Text note below.2o

prepare an analysis of economic impact which considers the costs and benefits to financial institutions, consumers, and other users of electronic fund transfers, including the extent to which additional documentation, reports, records, or other paper work would be required, and the effects upon competition in the provision of electronic banking services among large and small financial institutions and the availability of such services to different classes of consumers, particularly low income consumers,1
to the extent practicable, the Board shall demonstrate that the consumer protections of the proposed regulations outweigh the compliance costs imposed upon consumers and financial institutions, and1
any proposed regulations and accompanying analyses shall be sent promptly to Congress by the Board.1

children.heading: Issuance of model clauses
children.label: b Issuance of model clauses
children.text: The Bureau shall issue model clauses for optional use by financial institutions to facilitate compliance with the disclosure requirements of and to aid consumers in understanding the rights and responsibilities of participants in electronic fund transfers by utilizing readily understandable language. Such model clauses shall be adopted after notice duly given in the Federal Register and opportunity for public comment in accordance with . With respect to the disclosures required by section 1693c(a)(3) and (4) of this title, the Bureau shall take account of variations in the services and charges under different electronic fund transfer systems and, as appropriate, shall issue alternative model clauses for disclosure of these differing account terms.section 1693c of this titlesection 553 of title 5

children.heading: Criteria; modification of requirements
children.label: c Criteria; modification of requirements
children.text: Regulations prescribed hereunder may contain such classifications, differentiations, or other provisions, and may provide for such adjustments and exceptions for any class of electronic fund transfers or remittance transfers, as in the judgment of the Bureau are necessary or proper to effectuate the purposes of this subchapter, to prevent circumvention or evasion thereof, or to facilitate compliance therewith. The Bureau shall by regulation modify the requirements imposed by this subchapter on small financial institutions if the Bureau determines that such modifications are necessary to alleviate any undue compliance burden on small financial institutions and such modifications are consistent with the purpose and objective of this subchapter.

children.heading: Applicability to service providers other than certain financial institutions
children.label: d Applicability to service providers other than certain financial institutions

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: If electronic fund transfer services are made available to consumers by a person other than a financial institution holding a consumer’s account, the Bureau shall by regulation assure that the disclosures, protections, responsibilities, and remedies created by this subchapter are made applicable to such persons and services.

children.heading: State and local government electronic benefit transfer systems
children.label: 2 State and local government electronic benefit transfer systems
i
children.text: means a system under which a government agency distributes needs-tested benefits by establishing accounts that may be accessed by recipients electronically, such as through automated teller machines or point-of-sale terminals; and

children.label: ii
children.text: does not include employment-related payments, including salaries and pension, retirement, or unemployment benefits established by a Federal, State, or local government agency.

children.heading: Exemption generally
children.label: B Exemption generally
children.text: The disclosures, protections, responsibilities, and remedies established under this subchapter, and any regulation prescribed or order issued by the Bureau in accordance with this subchapter, shall not apply to any electronic benefit transfer system established under State or local law or administered by a State or local government.

children.heading: Exception for direct deposit into recipient’s account
children.label: C Exception for direct deposit into recipient’s account
children.text: Subparagraph (B) shall not apply with respect to any electronic funds transfer under an electronic benefit transfer system for a deposit directly into a consumer account held by the recipient of the benefit.

children.label: i
children.text: affects or alters the protections otherwise applicable with respect to benefits established by any other provision Federal, State, or local law; or3So in original. Probably should be followed by “of”.3

children.label: ii
children.text: otherwise supersedes the application of any State or local law.

children.heading: Fee disclosures at automated teller machines
children.label: 3 Fee disclosures at automated teller machines
i
children.text: the fact that a fee is imposed by such operator for providing the service; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the amount of any such fee.

children.heading: Notice requirement
children.label: B Notice requirement
children.text: The notice required under clauses (i) and (ii) of subparagraph (A) with respect to any fee described in such subparagraph shall appear on the screen of the automated teller machine, or on a paper notice issued from such machine, after the transaction is initiated and before the consumer is irrevocably committed to completing the transaction.

children.heading: Prohibition on fees not properly disclosed and explicitly assumed by consumer
children.label: C Prohibition on fees not properly disclosed and explicitly assumed by consumer
i
children.text: the consumer receives such notice in accordance with subparagraph (B); and

children.label: ii
children.text: the consumer elects to continue in the manner necessary to effect the transaction after receiving such notice.

children.label: I
children.text: operates an automated teller machine at which consumers initiate electronic fund transfers; and

children.label: II
children.text: is not the financial institution that holds the account of such consumer from which the transfer is made.

children.heading: Electronic fund transfer
children.label: ii Electronic fund transfer
children.text: The term “electronic fund transfer” includes a transaction that involves a balance inquiry initiated by a consumer in the same manner as an electronic fund transfer, whether or not the consumer initiates a transfer of funds in the course of the transaction.

children.heading: Host transfer services
children.label: iii Host transfer services
children.text: The term “host transfer services” means any electronic fund transfer made by an automated teller machine operator in connection with a transaction initiated by a consumer at an automated teller machine operated by such operator.
the Bureau in making determinations regarding the meaning or interpretation of any provision of this subchapter for which the Bureau has authority to prescribe regulations; or
the Board in making determinations regarding the meaning or interpretation of section 1693–2 of this title.o
